content.problemStatement:
Let’s define a function score:
Score(p[1...m],q[1...m])=∑i=1m(m−i+1)∗p[i]+q[i]Score(p[1...m], q[1...m]) = \sum_{i = 1}^{m} (m - i + 1) * p[i] + q[i]Score(p[1...m],q[1...m])=∑i=1m​(m−i+1)∗p[i]+q[i]

Let’s define Beauty of an array:
Beauty(p[1...n],q[1...n])=max1≤i≤j≤nScore(p[i...j],q[i...j])Beauty(p[1...n], q[1...n]) = max_{1 \le i \le j \le n} Score(p[i...j], q[i...j])Beauty(p[1...n],q[1...n])=max1≤i≤j≤n​Score(p[i...j],q[i...j])

Here p[i...j]p[i...j]p[i...j] means sub-array of ppp starting from iii-th position and ending at jjj-th position.

You are given two arrays AAA and BBB of nnn integers. You can do the following operation any number of times, possibly zero:


Perform a left circular rotation on **both** of the arrays simultaneously i.e move the first element of each array to the last position and shift all remaining elements one position left. For example, If an array is {1,5,3,2}\{1, 5, 3, 2\}{1,5,3,2} and you perform left circular rotation once, then the array will become {5,3,2,1}\{5, 3, 2, 1\}{5,3,2,1}. If you perform the operation again, the array will become {3,2,1,5}\{3, 2, 1, 5\}{3,2,1,5}.
You need to maximize the value Beauty(A,B)Beauty(A, B)Beauty(A,B).

Please note that, in one operation, you can't perform a left circular rotation on only one array, you must perform it on both of them.

**Note**: Because of large I/O, please use fast I/O methods.


Input Format

The first line of the input contains a single integer TTT denoting the number of test cases. The description of TTT test cases follows.
Each test case consists of 3 lines:

First line contains nnn, number of elements of the array.
Next line contains nnn space separated integers, denoting elements of the array AAA.
Next line contains nnn space separated integers, denoting elements of the array BBB.




Output Format
For each test case, output in a single line the answer for that test case, which is the maximum Beauty(A,B)Beauty(A, B)Beauty(A,B) that can be achieved.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
In the sample test, to get the maximum beauty value (7)(7)(7), you have to perform the left circular operation 222 times. Then the arrays will be:

A={−8,−1,4,−1,3}A = \{-8, -1, 4, -1, 3\}A={−8,−1,4,−1,3}
B={−2,3,−10,3,1}B = \{-2, 3, -10, 3, 1\}B={−2,3,−10,3,1}.

If we now pick i=3,j=5i = 3, j = 5i=3,j=5, we get Score(A[3...5],B[3...5])=7.Score(A[3...5], B[3...5]) = 7.Score(A[3...5],B[3...5])=7.
This is the maximum that we can obtain. So, Beauty(A,B)=7Beauty(A, B) = 7Beauty(A,B)=7.
